assertion_results.text: Test Quality verdict is WARN (not N/A or PASS) — the Eval Quality component is WARN because eval-3 has 2 failing assertions, and WARN in any component produces WARN in the combined Test Quality verdict
assertion_results.evidence: Report line 13: 'Test Quality | WARN | Repetitive Test Detection: N/A; Test Documentation: N/A; Eval Quality: WARN (54/56 assertions passed, 96% -- 2 regressions in eval-3 related to convention upgrade eligibility)'. The Test Quality verdict is WARN, driven by the Eval Quality component being WARN due to eval-3's 2 failing assertions (11/13, 85% pass rate).

assertion_results.text: The eval result review from github-actions[bot] is correctly identified as an eval result — the report's Test Quality or Eval Quality section reflects eval metrics (pass rates, failing assertions) extracted from the eval review, confirming the orchestrator detected and processed it
assertion_results.evidence: The report's Test Quality row (line 13) includes Eval Quality metrics: '54/56 assertions passed, 96%'. The Eval Quality Detail section (lines 48-65) shows per-eval pass rates (eval-1 through eval-5), baseline comparison ('eval-3 baseline is 14/14 (100%). PR eval-3 is 11/13 (85%)'), and specific failing assertion text. This confirms the orchestrator detected and processed the eval review, extracting pass rates and assertion details.

assertion_results.text: The human reviewer comment from reviewer-b (comment id 50001) is NOT misidentified as an eval result — it is processed as a normal review comment (classified in review-50001.md) and its classification does not reference eval detection or eval metrics
assertion_results.evidence: review-50001.md classifies reviewer-b's comment (id 50001) as 'code change request' with reasoning based on the comment's directive language, CHANGES_REQUESTED review state, and specific implementation guidance. The classification contains no references to eval detection, eval metrics, pass rates, or assertion results. It is processed entirely as a normal human review comment.

assertion_results.text: Eval pass rate and failing assertion details appear in the verification report's Test Quality row — the report includes eval-3's failing assertions about convention upgrade eligibility and sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: Report Test Quality row (line 13) states: 'Eval Quality: WARN (54/56 assertions passed, 96% -- 2 regressions in eval-3 related to convention upgrade eligibility)'. The Eval Quality Detail section (lines 61-65) lists both failing assertions: (1) 'Convention upgrade eligibility is not evaluated for review comment 30002 (index suggestion) -- no CONVENTIONS.md lookup or codebase pattern analysis documented in classification reasoning' and (2) 'No sub-task created for review comment 30002 -- classified as suggestion with no convention upgrade attempted'.

assertion_results.text: An eval failure sub-task is created for failing eval-3 — a sub-task description file targets eval-3 assertion failures and includes the failing assertion text and evidence
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-eval-3.md exists and targets eval-3 assertion failures. Its description (line 8): 'Fix eval-3 assertion failures related to convention upgrade eligibility evaluation and sub-task creation for review comment 30002'. The Review Context section (lines 33-41) includes both failing assertion texts and their evidence, quoting the specific assertion language and evidence about what was missing.

assertion_results.text: Sub-task descriptions include Review Context with the failing assertion text and evidence from the eval review — the Review Context section quotes the two failing assertions about convention upgrade eligibility and sub-task creation, including the evidence text explaining what was missing
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-eval-3.md lines 33-41 contain '## Review Context' with: Assertion 1: 'Convention upgrade eligibility is evaluated for review comment 30002 (index suggestion) -- the review classification output (review-30002.md) or the report\'s Style/Conventions analysis explains whether the suggestion matches a documented or demonstrated project convention' with Evidence: 'The output file review-30002.md classifies the comment as a suggestion but does not evaluate convention upgrade eligibility -- no CONVENTIONS.md lookup or codebase pattern analysis is documented in the classification reasoning'. Assertion 2: 'Review comment 30002 (index suggestion) results in a sub-task regardless of classification path...' with Evidence: 'No sub-task was created for review comment 30002 -- it was classified as suggestion and no convention upgrade was attempted, so the suggestion was not elevated to a code change request'.

assertion_results.text: Sub-task descriptions include a Target PR section pointing to the PR URL https://github.com/mrizzi/sdlc-plugins/pull/747 so that implement-task adds commits to the existing PR branch
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-eval-3.md lines 29-30: '## Target PR\nhttps://github.com/mrizzi/sdlc-plugins/pull/747'. subtask-50001.md lines 31-32: '## Target PR\nhttps://github.com/mrizzi/sdlc-plugins/pull/747'. Both sub-task descriptions include the Target PR section with the correct URL.

assertion_results.text: Root-cause investigation runs on the created eval failure sub-tasks — the report includes a Root-Cause Investigation verdict that is not N/A, indicating the investigation pipeline processed the eval failure sub-tasks
assertion_results.evidence: Report line 6: 'Root-Cause Investigation | N/A | Review feedback is a feature enhancement request, not a defect requiring root-cause investigation'. The Root-Cause Investigation verdict is N/A, indicating the investigation pipeline did NOT process the eval failure sub-tasks. The N/A reason references only the review feedback (feature enhancement), not the eval regression sub-task.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, creates sub-task descriptions, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The outputs directory contains only analysis and documentation files: report.md, review-50001.md, subtask-50001.md, subtask-eval-3.md, and criterion-1.md through criterion-7.md. All files are classification outputs, sub-task descriptions, or verification reports. No source code files (.ts, .js, .py, .rs, .go, .md skill files, etc.) appear in the outputs. No code modifications are indicated anywhere in the outputs.

assertion_results.text: Non-eval checks (Scope Containment, Sensitive Patterns, Acceptance Criteria) are unaffected by eval processing — Scope Containment compares the diff files against the task's Files to Modify, Sensitive Patterns scans the diff for secrets, and Acceptance Criteria evaluates each criterion against the diff content independently of the eval results
assertion_results.evidence: Report: Scope Containment (PASS): 'PR files match task-specified files exactly (2 files: style-conventions.md, SKILL.md)' — compares diff files against task spec. Sensitive Patterns (PASS): 'No secrets, credentials, or sensitive data patterns detected in added lines' — scans diff for secrets. Acceptance Criteria (PASS): '7/7 criteria met' — criterion-1.md through criterion-7.md each evaluate their criterion against the PR diff content (checking for specific sections, verdict definitions, mapping rows) with no references to eval results or eval processing. All three checks operate on the PR diff independently of the eval quality analysis.
